worth_it_explanation: Integral Ad Science (IAS) offers two distinct fraud-protection modes: a pre-bid avoidance segment that prevents the demand-side platform (DSP) from bidding on impressions IAS has flagged as invalid traffic (IVT), and a post-bid measurement tag that counts those impressions after they are bought and billed. An account running in post-bid mode only has IAS documenting the fraud exposure rather than preventing it. These checks confirm that pre-bid fraud avoidance segments are active on the right line items, that the avoidance stringency is consistent with post-bid findings, and that coverage is applied evenly across campaigns so no line items run unprotected.
intro: IAS pre-bid fraud avoidance segments work by sharing classification data with the DSP before a bid is placed. When the DSP receives a bid request, it queries IAS's fraud-detection signals for that impression. If IAS flags the impression as high-risk or moderate-risk for IVT, the DSP skips the auction rather than competing for it. This is distinct from IAS post-bid measurement, where the IAS tag fires on delivered impressions and classifies them after billing has already occurred. These checks focus on the pre-bid avoidance configuration: whether it is active, whether the fraud threshold matches what post-bid data shows about the account's actual IVT exposure, and whether coverage is consistent across all active line items.

audit_checks.title: IAS pre-bid fraud avoidance segments are not applied to DSP line items
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Without pre-bid fraud avoidance, the DSP bids on and delivers impressions that IAS would flag as invalid. Post-bid measurement records the IVT rate after those impressions are bought and billed. Every flagged impression in the post-bid report represents spend that pre-bid avoidance could have prevented from entering the auction at all.

educate.base:
IAS integrates with demand-side platforms (DSPs) in two ways. The first is a post-bid measurement tag that fires on delivered impressions and classifies each one for invalid traffic (IVT), brand safety, and viewability after the impression has been served and billed. The second is a pre-bid avoidance segment, a targeting exclusion applied in the DSP line item before the bid is placed, so the platform does not compete for impressions IAS has pre-classified as high-risk or moderate-risk for fraud.

The Media Rating Council (MRC) defines IVT as traffic that should not count in measurement because it is generated by nonhuman activity. The MRC separates IVT into two categories: general invalid traffic (GIVT), identified through routine means such as known bot lists and data-center signatures, and sophisticated invalid traffic (SIVT), which mimics real user behavior through mechanisms such as ad stacking, pixel stuffing, and bot networks that operate within real browser environments. IAS is MRC-accredited for both categories.

The distinction between pre-bid and post-bid matters because billing occurs at the point of delivery. An impression served against IVT is billed whether or not the post-bid tag later identifies it as invalid. Post-bid reporting surfaces the exposure; pre-bid avoidance prevents the bid from entering that auction in the first place, so the spend never reaches the flagged impression.

A few signs suggest an account is relying on post-bid measurement only:
  • IAS Signal shows IVT rate data for active campaigns, but no pre-bid segment appears in the DSP line item's targeting or brand safety settings
  • The DSP line item has an IAS measurement tag in its creative tracking section but no IAS avoidance segment ID in its targeting configuration
  • The IAS account manager or DSP support confirms that the integration is set to 'verification' or 'measurement' mode only
You may want to check the line item's targeting configuration in the DSP directly rather than relying on the IAS Signal dashboard alone. Both pre-bid and post-bid modes generate impression data in IAS Signal, so the presence of data in the dashboard does not confirm that pre-bid avoidance is active.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser running programmatic display or video, IVT inflates impression delivery counts without producing any shopping signal. Pre-bid avoidance keeps invalid impressions out of the auction entirely, so the delivery data that feeds line-item optimization reflects real users rather than bot traffic.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, IVT on the buy does not produce conversions, but it does consume budget and inflate delivery numbers. Pre-bid avoidance reduces the share of spend reaching invalid impressions. You may want to compare delivery-to-conversion rates before and after activating avoidance to confirm the change in lead outcomes.

Enabling pre-bid IAS segments in an ad set

educate.learn_more_note: IAS Signal setup guides for activating pre-bid fraud avoidance segments within specific DSPs are available through the IAS help center at helpcenter.integralplatform.com. Access requires an IAS partner login. Your IAS account manager can provide direct links to the DSP-specific activation guide for your platform.

investigate.intro: Confirm, for each active DSP line item, whether an IAS pre-bid fraud avoidance segment is attached as a targeting exclusion or whether only a post-bid measurement tag is present.

ias_signal.label: IAS Signal
steps: Log in to IAS Signal and navigate to the Campaigns view for the advertiser you are auditing.

For each active campaign, look for an indication in the campaign setup that a pre-bid segment is applied. IAS Signal distinguishes between campaigns configured for pre-bid targeting and those running post-bid measurement only.
Note the fraud avoidance threshold applied to any pre-bid segments: IAS typically offers high-risk-only avoidance and high-plus-moderate-risk avoidance. Confirm which level is active.

dsp_ui.label: DSP UI (your DSP: e.g., DV360, The Trade Desk, Quantcast, or similar)
steps: Open the DSP and navigate to the line item or ad set you want to check.

Open the line item's targeting or brand safety settings. Look for an IAS pre-bid segment ID applied as an exclusion. In DV360, this appears under the brand safety section of the line item; in other DSPs it may appear under 'Third-Party Brand Safety' or 'Pre-bid Targeting.'

Confirm whether an IAS fraud avoidance segment ID appears in the targeting configuration. An IAS measurement tag in the creative's tracking section is separate from a pre-bid targeting segment and does not constitute pre-bid avoidance.
Repeat for each active line item. Line items within the same campaign may have different configurations if they were set up at different times or by different team members.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active line item, whether an IAS pre-bid fraud avoidance segment is attached in the DSP targeting configuration. A line item with a pre-bid segment actively applied as a targeting exclusion passes. A line item with only an IAS measurement tag and no pre-bid avoidance segment is recorded as a fail, with the line item name, the campaign it belongs to, and the DSP noted.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the targeting configuration for each active line item above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that IAS pre-bid fraud avoidance segments are not applied to {{site_name}}'s DSP line items. IAS is connected to the account and generating post-bid IVT measurement data, but the pre-bid segment that would prevent the DSP from bidding on those impressions before billing occurs is not attached to the line item targeting. Every impression the post-bid report flags as invalid represents spend that was paid before the classification happened. {{voice_recommend}} attaching IAS pre-bid fraud avoidance segments as targeting exclusions on each active line item within the DSP, and confirming the segment configuration in both the DSP's targeting settings and IAS Signal, to stop invalid impressions from entering the auction rather than counting them after delivery.

audit_checks.title: IAS pre-bid fraud avoidance threshold does not match the account's post-bid IVT findings
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A pre-bid threshold set to avoid only high-risk impressions leaves moderate-risk IVT passing through to delivery. If post-bid measurement consistently reports elevated moderate-risk IVT, the pre-bid configuration is not providing the level of protection the account's own data supports. Conversely, a threshold blocking both high- and moderate-risk impressions on low-fraud-risk inventory reduces reach without corresponding protection benefit.

educate.base:
IAS pre-bid fraud avoidance segments are available at two thresholds. The first blocks only impressions IAS classifies as high risk for invalid traffic (IVT). The second blocks both high-risk and moderate-risk impressions. The threshold choice affects two things: how much IVT reaches delivery, and how much eligible inventory the pre-bid segment removes from the available pool before the DSP bids.

Post-bid IAS measurement assigns each delivered impression a fraud risk classification: high risk, moderate risk, or low risk. An account's post-bid data shows the distribution of IVT classifications across the delivered buy. If post-bid reports consistently show a material share of moderate-risk IVT, but the pre-bid threshold is set to block only high-risk impressions, the avoidance configuration is less stringent than the post-bid findings would support.

The reverse also applies. An account running in a low-fraud-risk environment, where post-bid data shows minimal IVT across all risk levels, may be blocking moderate-risk impressions pre-bid and reducing available inventory beyond what the actual fraud exposure warrants. The pre-bid threshold should reflect what the account's own post-bid data shows about IVT distribution on that specific buy.

A few signs suggest the threshold may be mismatched:
  • Post-bid IAS Signal reports show a consistent moderate-risk IVT rate above the industry average, but the pre-bid segment is set to high-risk avoidance only
  • The pre-bid and post-bid IVT rates in IAS Signal diverge significantly, with post-bid rates materially higher than what pre-bid avoidance would predict given the threshold applied
  • The threshold was set at account setup and has not been reviewed against actual post-bid findings from the same inventory sources
You may want to compare the pre-bid threshold setting directly against the last 90 days of post-bid IVT rate data in IAS Signal before deciding whether an adjustment is warranted.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser running campaigns across multiple DSPs, IVT risk distribution can vary by inventory source. You may want to review post-bid IVT findings by DSP or supply source separately rather than treating the account as a single pool. The appropriate threshold may differ across channels.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser where each conversion has measurable value, moderate-risk IVT that reaches delivery consumes budget without producing leads. You may want to compare the cost of blocking moderate-risk impressions pre-bid, measured as reduced reach, against the share of moderate-risk IVT in post-bid reports to determine whether tightening the threshold is justified.

About Integral Ad Science's media quality verification in Display and Video 360

educate.learn_more_note: IAS Signal's fraud avoidance threshold settings and the per-campaign pre-bid vs. post-bid comparison reports are documented in the IAS help center at helpcenter.integralplatform.com. Access requires an IAS partner login. Your IAS account manager can walk through threshold calibration options for your specific inventory mix.

investigate.intro: Pull the pre-bid fraud avoidance threshold setting from IAS Signal or the DSP, then compare it against 90 days of post-bid IVT rate data to assess whether the threshold reflects actual IVT exposure.

ias_signal.label: IAS Signal
steps: Log in to IAS Signal and navigate to the campaign or advertiser view.
Locate the pre-bid segment configuration for each active campaign. Note the fraud avoidance threshold: high-risk-only, or high-plus-moderate-risk.

Pull a post-bid IVT report for the same campaigns covering the past 90 days. Note the IVT rate by risk level: what share of delivered impressions are classified high-risk IVT, moderate-risk IVT, and low-risk or clean.

Compare the pre-bid threshold against the post-bid IVT distribution. If moderate-risk IVT represents a material share of post-bid findings and the pre-bid threshold blocks only high-risk, note that as a mismatch.

dsp_ui.label: DSP UI (your DSP: e.g., DV360, The Trade Desk, Quantcast, or similar)
steps: In the DSP, open the line item's targeting or brand safety settings and confirm the IAS fraud avoidance segment ID applied.
Cross-reference the segment ID against the IAS Signal segment catalog to confirm which risk threshold it corresponds to: high-risk-only or high-plus-moderate-risk.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per campaign, the pre-bid avoidance threshold and the 90-day post-bid moderate-risk and high-risk IVT rates from IAS Signal. A campaign where the pre-bid threshold matches the post-bid IVT risk distribution, or where a documented rationale supports the threshold choice, passes. A campaign where post-bid data shows a material moderate-risk IVT rate and the pre-bid threshold blocks only high-risk is recorded as a mismatch, with the campaign name, the threshold applied, and the post-bid moderate-risk IVT rate noted.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the pre-bid threshold and the post-bid IVT rate by risk level for each campaign above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that the IAS pre-bid fraud avoidance threshold applied to {{site_name}}'s campaigns does not align with what the account's own post-bid IVT data shows. Post-bid IAS Signal reports indicate a material share of moderate-risk invalid traffic reaching delivery, while the pre-bid configuration is set to block only high-risk impressions. The avoidance segment is active but calibrated at a level the post-bid findings do not support. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the 90-day post-bid IVT rate breakdown in IAS Signal by risk level, comparing that distribution against the current pre-bid threshold, and adjusting the segment to the threshold that reflects the account's actual IVT exposure rather than leaving the configuration at the default set during initial integration.

audit_checks.title: IAS pre-bid fraud avoidance is applied inconsistently, leaving some line items without coverage
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A pre-bid fraud avoidance segment applied to some line items but not others produces uneven protection across the same campaign. Line items without the segment receive no pre-bid filtering, so fraud exposure on those lines is not prevented regardless of the configuration on adjacent lines. The IVT rate for the campaign as a whole can look acceptable while specific line items accumulate significant fraud exposure.

educate.base:
IAS pre-bid fraud avoidance segments are configured at the line-item level in most demand-side platforms (DSPs). An advertiser with 20 active line items across a campaign may have the IAS pre-bid segment applied to 15 of them while 5 run without it. At the campaign level, IAS Signal may show an IVT rate that looks within range because the 15 protected lines are pulling the average down. The 5 unprotected lines may have materially higher IVT rates that the aggregate figure masks.

Uneven coverage develops in a few ways: line items added to a campaign mid-flight may not inherit the brand safety configuration from the existing lines, line items created by different team members may follow different setup checklists, and line items targeting different inventory types, such as connected TV (CTV) versus display, may have been configured separately without applying the same verification setup.

A few signs point to inconsistent coverage:
  • IAS Signal shows campaign-level IVT data, but some line items within the campaign show no IAS impression data for the same date range
  • Post-bid IVT rates vary significantly across line items within the same campaign, with some lines showing much higher fraud rates than others
  • The DSP shows the IAS pre-bid segment in the targeting settings for some line items but not all within the same insertion order
You may want to review pre-bid segment coverage at the line-item level rather than the campaign level. Campaign-level averages can obscure gaps at the individual line item.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser running separate line items by product category, audience, or device type, uneven IAS coverage can mean that a high-spend line targeting a specific segment runs without fraud protection while lower-spend lines are protected. You may want to prioritize checking coverage on the highest-spend lines first.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, a line item without pre-bid fraud protection that also happens to be a high-frequency delivery vehicle can accumulate a disproportionate share of IVT impressions. You may want to cross-reference line-item-level IVT rates in IAS Signal against the line items that drove the highest delivery volumes in the DSP.

learn_more.source_title: About Integral Ad Science's media quality verification in Display and Video 360
Enabling pre-bid IAS segments in an ad set

educate.learn_more_note: IAS Signal's campaign and line-item coverage views, which show which line items have pre-bid segments active vs. measurement-only, are documented in the IAS help center at helpcenter.integralplatform.com. Access requires an IAS partner login.

investigate.intro: Confirm, for every active line item in the campaigns under review, whether an IAS pre-bid fraud avoidance segment is present in the DSP targeting configuration, and cross-reference against IAS Signal to identify any line items missing coverage.

ias_signal.label: IAS Signal
steps: Log in to IAS Signal and navigate to the campaign view for the advertiser you are auditing.

Within each campaign, look for a line-item or ad set breakdown. Identify any line items that show no IAS impression data for the current flight period, which may indicate no IAS measurement or pre-bid segment is active on those lines.

Compare the line-item-level IVT rates across the campaign. A wide spread, with some lines showing elevated fraud rates and others showing near-zero, may indicate that unprotected lines are receiving more IVT.

dsp_ui.label: DSP UI (your DSP: e.g., DV360, The Trade Desk, Quantcast, or similar)
steps: In the DSP, open the insertion order or campaign and view the full list of active line items.
For each line item, open the targeting or brand safety settings and confirm whether an IAS pre-bid fraud avoidance segment ID is present.

Build a simple list of line items with the pre-bid segment applied and line items without it. Any line item without the segment is exposed to fraud that the IAS configuration on the rest of the campaign is preventing.
Pay particular attention to line items added mid-flight or after the initial campaign setup, as those are the lines most likely to have been created without the pre-bid segment.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active line item, whether an IAS pre-bid fraud avoidance segment is present in the DSP targeting. A campaign where all active line items have the pre-bid segment applied passes. A campaign where one or more active line items lack the pre-bid segment is a fail, recorded with the names of the unprotected line items, the insertion order they belong to, and the date range they have been running without coverage.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the pre-bid segment status for each active line item above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that IAS pre-bid fraud avoidance coverage is inconsistent across {{site_name}}'s active line items. Some line items within the same campaign have the IAS pre-bid segment applied as a targeting exclusion, while others are running without it and receiving no pre-bid fraud filtering. IVT on the unprotected lines reaches delivery and is billed, regardless of what the protected lines are filtering out. {{voice_recommend}} auditing every active line item in the DSP against the IAS pre-bid segment configuration, applying the avoidance segment to all lines that are currently unprotected, and adding a pre-bid segment verification step to the new-line-item setup checklist so future line items do not launch without coverage.
